title: The Fifth Sunday after the Epiphany at the Wilderness
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: February 6, 2022

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Fifth Sunday after the Epiphany in the Christian calendar, falling during Ordinary Time in the liturgical year. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday would have included Isaiah 40:21-31, Psalm 147:1-11, 20c, 1 Corinthians 9:16-23, and Mark 1:29-39, emphasizing healing and Christ's withdrawal to pray in wilderness places. The sermon title "The Fifth Sunday after the Epiphany at the Wilderness" directly references the Mark passage about Jesus healing Simon's mother-in-law and withdrawing to solitary places to pray.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the midst of preparations for General Convention scheduled for summer 2022 in Baltimore, where major debates over human sexuality and ordination standards were anticipated. In the Diocese of Colorado, Bishop Kee Sloan had been serving since 2015 and the diocese was navigating pandemic recovery issues affecting cathedral attendance and operations. No major Episcopal controversies or bishop elections were occurring at this immediate moment, though the broader church remained divided over progressive versus traditional stances on LGBTQ+ inclusion.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing mild winter weather in early February 2022 with temperatures in the 40s-50s Fahrenheit, a significant thaw after January cold snaps. Mayor Michael Hancock's administration was managing ongoing downtown revitalization efforts and homeless services challenges in Capitol Hill, where the cathedral is located. The Colorado State Legislature was in its annual session preparing budget negotiations and healthcare policy discussions that would dominate the month.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The dominant national story was accelerating inflation, with the Consumer Price Index reaching a 40-year high of 7% year-over-year, driving political debates about Federal Reserve policy and economic management heading toward midterm elections. The U.S. was heavily focused on the brewing Ukraine crisis, with Russian military buildups on the border and President Biden warning of invasion risk while threatening severe sanctions. The Supreme Court was hearing arguments in Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization (the case that would overturn Roe v. Wade in June), with major abortion rights activism and counter-activism occurring nationwide.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia was massing over 100,000 troops on the Ukrainian border, with Western intelligence warning an invasion could occur within weeks; the U.S. and EU were coordinating sanctions threats while Russia denied invasion plans. China was preparing for the Beijing Winter Olympics opening ceremony on February 4, 2022, just two days before this sermon, bringing international attention to Beijing and human rights concerns regarding Xinjiang detention camps. Western nations were engaged in diplomatic boycotts of the Olympics opening ceremony over these human rights concerns.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was gripped by anxiety about geopolitical instability (Ukraine, China, Russia), economic uncertainty from inflation, and persistent pandemic fatigue nearly two years into COVID-19, with vaccination debates still deeply polarizing. The Super Bowl LVI had just occurred on February 13, 2022 (one week after this sermon), building anticipation in Denver sports culture despite the Broncos' disappointing season. There was a general cultural mood of exhaustion and division, making the sermon's focus on Christ's wilderness prayer and healing ministry potentially resonant with congregants seeking spiritual grounding amid turbulent times.
